--- title: "ACQUIRE" subtitle: "Acquisition, Curation and QUality Infrastructure for REproducible in-the-wild studies" toc: false page-layout: full --- ::: {.acq-hero} ::: {.acq-lede} Reproducibility begins at acquisition. ::: Most in-the-wild sensing studies collect invalid data for weeks before anyone notices. Dashboards stay green, files keep arriving, and the failure only surfaces during analysis — when the fieldwork is already spent. ACQUIRE is a guideline book and a set of diagnostics for catching those failures while they are still cheap to fix. ::: {.acq-cta} [Building a study? Start here](lifecycle/index.qmd) [Something wrong with your data?](recipes/index.qmd){.secondary} ::: ::: ## What silent failure looks like {.unnumbered} Below is one hour of accelerometer data from a phone that reported itself healthy for the entire recording. The app requested 50 Hz. Every file arrived. No error was logged. ::: {#cell-fig-hero .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Effective sampling rate collapses halfway through the recording. Nothing in the application's own telemetry registers this.](index_files/figure-html/fig-hero-output-1.png){#fig-hero width=931 height=267} ::: ::: Running the diagnostics against it takes one line: ::: {#94152ace .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import acquire report = acquire.check(df, nominal_hz=50) report ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
✓Timestamp monotonicity0 non-increasing steps0
×Effective vs nominal sampling rate38.41 Hz (77% of nominal)50.00 Hz ±5%recipe →
×Stream continuity1 gaps, 45.1 s lost, longest 45.1 sno interval > 1.0 srecipe →
×Sampling regularityjitter 100.0% above median interval (p90)≤ 25%recipe →
✓Resting magnitude9.806 m/s² (1.000 g)9.807 m/s² ±2%
✓Stuck sensorlongest frozen run 0.0 s≤ 2.0 s
✓Saturation0.001% of samples at rail (±15.6 m/s²)≤ 0.1%
``` ::: ::: Two failures, both invisible to conventional monitoring, both traceable to a specific recipe. That is the whole idea. ## Two ways in {.unnumbered} **[The guideline book](lifecycle/index.qmd)** follows the study lifecycle — design, instrument, pilot, deploy, monitor, curate, release. Each stage carries an ordered checklist. Use it when you are building a study and want to know what to do next. **[The failure catalogue](recipes/index.qmd)** is entered by symptom. Each entry states what you observe, what causes it, how to detect it automatically, and how well that detection is evidenced. Use it when your data already looks wrong. ## Run it on your own data {.unnumbered} The diagnostics are a Python package, intended to run in your own pipeline or CI against incoming study data: ```bash pip install acquire-framework acquire check recordings/day01.csv --nominal 50 ``` It exits non-zero on failure, so it can gate a pipeline. There is also a [browser-based demonstration](check/index.qmd) that runs the same code locally in your tab — nothing is uploaded, and nothing leaves your device. ## Status {.unnumbered} Version 0.1.0. The catalogue is small and honest about it: every recipe carries an evidence level, and most are currently at the lowest one. Replications are the single most useful contribution — see [contributing](https://github.com/acquire-framework/acquire-framework.github.io/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
